Xiaomi recruits senior talent for European EV research centre
data.description:
China's Xiaomi has hired at least five senior executives from firms including Germany's BMW, LinkedIn posts showed, as it advances plans to build an electric vehicle research and development centre in Europe.

The world's third largest smartphone maker began manufacturing EVs last year with the launch of the SU7 sedan after selling phones, household appliances and smart gadgets for most of its 15-year history.

It has since become one of the biggest disrupters in the Chinese auto market, outselling Tesla's Model 3 since December, with company president Lu Weibing saying this week that it will begin selling its cars abroad in 2027.

Xiaomi hired Rudolf Dittrich, a 15-year BMW veteran, last year to head the planned research centre, according to his LinkedIn profile.

Other new hires include Dusan Sarac, a former manager at BMW Group , who said on his LinkedIn profile that he had joined the European research centre this month. Jannis Hellwig also joined Xiaomi from BMW and will serve as a senior engineer on performance development and integration.

BMW spokespeople did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

Xiaomi is also hiring for several other positions to be based in Munich, Germany, including senior automotive designer, senior automotive exterior designer and vehicle dynamics engineers, according to LinkedIn jobs page.

The company told Reuters on Friday that its EV research and development centre in Europe is "in the process of planning." It gave no details on the facility's planned location, when it will open or the size of Xiaomi's investment.

Company president Lu did not elaborate on which markets it plans to target when it launches sales outside China.

data.title: Nestle's strength now reflected in share price - RBC cuts to 'sector perform'
data.description:
** RBC cuts packaged food giant Nestle to "sector perform" from "outperform", saying the inherent strength of its competitive position is now reflected in the share price that is approaching the broker's PT of CHF 93

** "Our views on the inherent strength of Nestlé's competitive position, and belief that management is doing the right thing, is unchanged, but this is now realistically reflected in the share price," it says

** The broker adds its estimates are in line with consensus and the company's guidance, which in its view has become "more realistic"

** Out of 26 analysts that cover Nestle, 11 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy",​ 13 "hold" and two​ "strong sell" or "sell" - LSEG

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: British Telco BT Eyes Potential Partnerships for International Unit
data.description:
(Updates to add statements from BT and Orange)

BT Group (BT-A.L) reportedly reached out to telecommunications companies AT&T and Orange to explore potential partnerships involving its struggling international business.

The British telco could sell a stake in the unit, Bloomberg News reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter. However, talks are at a preliminary stage and a deal might not materialize.

BT told MT Newswires "We have been clear that we are looking at options to optimise our international operations. Although we would never comment on specific rumour and speculation, as you would expect we're keeping everything open and this means we've been speaking to third parties about a range of possibilities."

Orange said it does not comment on market rumors, while AT&T, did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

Slovenia Producer Prices Rebounds in February
data.description:
Producer prices in Slovenia rose by 0.5% year-on-year in February 2025, recovering from a 0.8% drop in the previous month.

This marked the first growth in producer prices since November 2023, as costs rebounded for mining and quarrying (2.4% vs -0.3% in January) and manufacturing (0.7% vs -0.6%).

At the same time, producer inflation increased further for water supply (5.8% vs 3.8%), while it declined at a softer pace for electricity, gas, steam and air-conditioning supply (-4.8% vs -7.1%).

On a monthly basis, producer prices advanced by 0.4% in February, following a 0.5% fall in the preceding period.

data.title: Aiforia Technologies, Techcyte Team Up for AI Digital Pathology Platform
data.description:
Aiforia Technologies and Techcyte are partnering to develop artificial intelligence-driven workflows for anatomic pathology laboratories to improve efficiency, accuracy, and patient outcomes.

Under the partnership, the Finnish medical software company will integrate its AI diagnostic applications, starting with its breast cancer suite, into the workflow of Techcyte's Fusion digital pathology platform, according to a Friday release.

data.title: REG-Net Asset Value(s)
data.description:
Octopus Future Generations VCT plc

Net Asset Value

The Board of Octopus Future Generations VCT plc (the ‘Company’) has reviewed the portfolio and announces that as at 31 December 2024, the unaudited Net Asset Value (‘NAV’) of the Company was 88.8 pence per share.

The movement in NAV over the six months has been driven by some modest increases in portfolio company valuations. This is reflective of businesses which have successfully concluded further funding rounds, grown revenues or met certain important milestones. Further details will be provided in the results for the 18 month period ended 31 December 2024 due to be released in late April.

BOCOM International's 2024 Loss Narrows; Revenue Slips
data.description:
BOCOM International's attributable loss narrowed to HK$1.23 billion in 2024 from HK$1.47 billion in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Loss per share at the financial services company narrowed to HK$0.45 from HK$0.54 in the previous year.

Revenue fell to HK$387.5 million from HK$613.2 million a year earlier.

data.title: Russian drones hammer Ukraine's Odesa as Czech leader visits
data.description:
Russia pounded Ukraine's Black Sea city of Odesa with one of its biggest drone attacks late on Thursday, injuring three teenagers and sparking fires as the Czech president visited, Ukrainian officials said.

The attack comes as the United States is pushing for a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia, and hoping to agree a partial ceasefire that would halt strikes on energy infrastructure by both sides.

Czech President Petr Pavel, a vocal backer of Kyiv who has led an effort to source more than one million artillery shells for Ukraine's war effort, was in the port city for talks with regional officials at the time of the strikes.

"Significantly, it was during our meeting that the enemy once again massively attacked the Odesa region," Governor Oleh Kiper said on the Telegram messaging app.

The long-range drones buzzed into the city in several waves, damaging infrastructure, residential houses and commercial buildings, and causing multiple fires, the interior ministry said.

Oleksandr Kovalenko, a military analyst, told Reuters that Russia used new tactics for the attack, having its drones descend from a higher altitude than usual and at high speeds to make it harder for Ukraine's air defences.

He said it was one of the "most massive" attacks on Odesa since Russia invaded in February 2022: "It was intimidation. Terror against the civilian population."

Both Russia and Ukraine have agreed during separate talks with U.S. officials that they are ready for a moratorium on strikes on energy infrastructure. Moscow rejected a more comprehensive 30-day ceasefire.

Ukrainian, U.S. and Russian delegations are due to meet in Saudi Arabia separately on Monday to discuss the details, officials have said.

Russia launched a total of 214 drones at Ukraine overnight, the air force said. It did not specify how many drones targeted Odesa specifically.

The air force said it shot down 114 of the drones and that and another 81 drones were "lost", its term for those suppressed using electronic warfare defences.

Ukraine has used drones to continue striking targets in Russia, hitting oil infrastructure and a strategic bomber base in recent days.

data.title: Greece arrests state employees on Rhodes as concerns over bribery grow
data.description:
Greece has charged more than 20 people and arrested five managers and employees at the state town planning service on the tourist island of Rhodes, for violations ranging from breach of duty to bribery and document forgery, police said on Thursday.

It is the second scandal to emerge since December involving public sector workers accused of bribery over the issuance of construction permits, and follows years of economic hardship and international bailouts for the country.

Transparency International, which measures perceptions of public sector corruption, ranks Greece second from bottom in the euro zone ahead of Malta.

The Greek police investigation was launched after a member of the public tipped them off that town planning employees were asking for bribes for related work of up to 100,000 euros.

In total seven people have been arrested including an architect, who police believe is the leader of the group involved, and a civil engineer.

More than 600,000 euros ($650,000) and 77 gold British pounds, worth hundreds of euros each, were confiscated from the homes of those detained, among them two senior managers, police officials said.

In December, 21 people were arrested in northern Greece including a former mayor and municipal managers at a local town planning service on similar charges. They were accused of receiving bribes to expedite the issuance of permits and topographical plans for illegal construction work.

($1 = 0.9230 euros)

data.title: FedEx shares drop as annual forecast cuts stoke worries on economy
data.description:
FedEx's shares fell sharply after the parcel delivery firm cut its fiscal 2025 forecasts, adding to worries about the health of the U.S. industrial economy against the backdrop of the Trump administration's sweeping tariffs on trading partners.

The company's stock was down 6% before the bell on Friday after CEO Raj Subramaniam said the day before that FedEx was "navigating a very challenging operating environment."

Rival UPS' shares also fell 1.3% premarket.

U.S. President Donald Trump's import tariffs have created uncertainty for businesses, prompting them to be more cautious with their spending as they navigate an uncertain economic landscape.

Both FedEx and UPS are viewed as barometers for the global economy due to their involvement with a wide range of industries.

Shipments from companies that produce goods used in manufacturing drive substantial cargo volumes and high-margin deliveries for the delivery firms.

Analysts and economists have said that Trump's import levies could trigger a recession and a trade war, further affecting transportation and delivery demand.

"FedEx's Q3 print and full-year forecast cut will likely exacerbate concerns of structural pressures in the parcel business," Morgan Stanley said, adding that it may even overwhelm the company's cost-cutting program.

FedEx has been reducing costs as demand for lower-margin e-commerce deliveries from companies such as Temu and Shein outpaces higher-margin business-to-business shipments.

"Management noted weakness in the industrial economy and, while macro is a factor, we believe structural forces are a far bigger headwind than the market thinks," Morgan Stanley added.

Memphis-based FedEx lowered its fiscal 2025 profit forecast on Thursday and now expects adjusted earnings per share between $18.00 and $18.60, compared with its previous outlook of $19 to $20.

The company also expects revenue for the 12 months ending May to be flat to slightly down year-on-year, versus its earlier forecast for it to be about flat.

data.title: Polystyrene (PS) prices trend higher in Europe
data.description: This week, PS prices marched higher in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, " Polystyrene (PS) prices have risen following the announcement of the permanent closure of a propylene oxide and styrene monomer (POSM) production facility in the Netherlands, which has raised concerns about possible supply disruptions and price fluctuations in the European market. Two prominent multinational chemical companies have confirmed the shutdown of their POSM production site located in Maasvlakte, Netherlands. Market observers have indicated that this development could increase volatility in the upstream styrene monomer (SM) market, particularly if any unscheduled outages occur in the future."The source added, "Conversely, some industry stakeholders have minimized the potential repercussions, arguing that the closure would have a negligible impact on the PS market due to the overall low production levels. Throughout the week, the demand for polystyrene remained steady, with adequate supply available to satisfy current market requirements. Although demand from the construction sector was weak, the packaging industry continued to bolster the consumption of general-purpose polystyrene. The sluggish activity in the new building and insulation sectors was reflected in the pricing of finished goods, which had fallen to very low levels. Meanwhile, contract prices exhibited a more robust trend for the month, although reports indicated that one producer had proposed contract price increases that were lower than the monomer contract price hike in an effort to stimulate demand. Import volumes into Europe remained constrained, adding further uncertainty to the supply dynamics. In the spot markets, GPPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1565-1575/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, a week on week rise of Euro (+20/mt), while HIPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1665-1675/mt FD North West Europe levels, a gain of Euro (+20/mt) from last week. EPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1740-1750/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, stable week on week.In the contract markets, GPPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1670-1675/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both rising by Euro (+20/mt) from last week. GPPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1670-1675/mt FD NWE France levels, higher by Euro (+20/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, GPPS prices were assessed at the GBP 1400-1405/mt FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+15/mt) week on week. In the contract markets, HIPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1770-1775/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both gaining by Euro (+20/mt) from last week. HIPS prices were also assessed at the Euro 1770-1775/mt FD NWE France levels, a week on week increase of Euro (+20/mt). Meanwhile, HIPS prices were assessed at the GBP 1485-1490/m FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+15/mt) from the previous week.In the contract markets, EPS prices were assessed at the Euro 2140-2145/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both higher by Euro (+20/mt) from last week. EPS prices were assessed at the Euro 2140-2145/mt FD NWE France levels, an increase of Euro (+20/mt) from last week. Meanwhile, EPS prices were assessed at the GBP 1795-1800/mt FD NWE UK level, up GBP (+15/mt) from last week.Upstream SM spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the USD 1210-1220/mt FOB Rotterdam levels, a sharp decline of USD (-40/mt) from the previous week.European Styrene contract price for March 2025 has been settled at the Euro 1554/mt CIF North West Europe. This price represents a sharp gain of Euro 65/mt from its February 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Earnings Flash (NIO) Nio Posts Q4 Total Revenue 19.70B Chinese Renminbi, vs. FactSet Est of 20.24B Renminbi
Albania approves construction of PV plant near Kukes airport
data.description:
TIRANA (Albania), March 21 (SeeNews) - The Albanian government said it has approved a request by Kukes Airport's owner Global Technical Mechanics and local construction firm Bami to develop a 12-MW photovoltaic (PV) plant in the airport's area in the north-eastern village of Shtiqen.

The plant is expected to become operational within three years, the government said in a press release on Thursday.

The new project builds upon the existing concession contract signed between the government and the Global Technical Mechanics-Bami consortium in 2019, aiming to turn an existing airfield into an alternative airport to Albania’s main airport in Tirana.

Kukes International Airport Zayed, built with investments from United Arab Emirates' company Emaar Properties, operated its first flight in April 2021. The airport was closed in 2023 following the withdrawal of Hungarian low-cost carrier Wizz Air just a year after starting operations there. This left the facility with only a few flights operated by Turkish-Albanian company Air Albania, which subsequently also suspended operations.

In December, Albanian air traffic operator Albcontrol launched a tender for a feasibility study on Kukes airport's air traffic infrastructure. The study will assess cost-effective solutions, system harmonisation, and the feasibility of managing air traffic control from a single integrated centre.

data.title: REG-Stolt-Nielsen Limited Distributes Material for the 2025 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
data.description:
LONDON, March 21, 2025 – Stolt-Nielsen Limited (Oslo Børs: SNI) announced today that materials for the Company's Annual General Meeting have been made available. Shareholders of record as at close of business on March 20, 2025 will be entitled to vote at the meeting. Shareholders who have elected for a physical copy of the materials will receive these by post. Copies of such materials are available at: stolt-nielsen.com/investors/annual-general-meeting/

The Company's Annual General Meeting of Shareholders will be held at the registered office of the Company at Clarendon House, 2 Church Street, Hamilton HM 11, Bermuda, on Thursday, April 17, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. local time. Proxy votes must be received by DNB Bank ASA, Registrars Dept., P.O. Box 1600 Sentrum, 0021 Oslo, Norway, or at: vote@dnb.no, not later than April 15, 2025 12:00 hours Central European Summer Time. We strongly encourage shareholders to email scanned copies of their proxies to: vote@dnb.no before the deadline noted above.

For additional information please contact:

data.title: REG - Paradigm Homes C.H. - Paradigm plan to buy almost 3,500 homes from L&Q
data.description:
RNS Number : 4920B Paradigm Homes Charitable Housing 21 March 2025  

Paradigm Housing proposal to acquire almost 3,500 homes in South Buckinghamshire from L&Q

Paradigm Homes Charitable Housing Association Limited has announced its intention to acquire nearly 3,500 homes in South Buckinghamshire from L&Q.

The proposed acquisition accompanies the announcement today that Paradigm Housing Group and Settle Group are in discussions to formally explore a partnership. If both proposals go ahead, the new association will have 30,000 homes and be the largest across the Bedfordshire, Buckinghamshire and Hertfordshire area.

The partnership will be subject to registration by the Financial Conduct Authority.

Paradigm is registered as a charitable community benefit society under the Co-operative and Community Benefit Society Act 2014 with registered number 28844R and as a registered provider with the Social Housing Regulator with registered number L4215.

The Issuer is registered as a charitable community benefit society under the Co-operative and Community Benefit Society Act 2014 with registered number 27789R and as a registered provider with the Social Housing Regulator with registered number LH4138.

Settle is registered as a charitable community benefit society under the Co-operative and Community Benefit Society Act 2014 with registered number 30003R and as a registered provider with the Social Housing Regulator with registered number L4370.

For further information, please contact:

Nicola Ewen

Paradigm Homes Charitable Housing Limited

Wooburn Green

data.title: REG - Paradigm Homes C.H. - Paradigm and Settle Enter Partnership Discussions
data.description:
RNS Number : 4847B Paradigm Homes Charitable Housing 21 March 2025  

Paradigm Housing Group Limited & Settle Group enter partnership discussions

Paradigm Homes Charitable Housing Association Limited (the Issuer)

£300,000,000 5.25 per cent. Secured Sustainability Bonds due 2044; and

£350,000,000 2.25 per cent. Secured Bonds due 2051

(the Bonds)

The boards of Paradigm Housing Group Limited (Paradigm) and Settle Group (Settle) have agreed to enter into discussions with a view to forming a legal partnership.

The decision was agreed by their respective boards this week. The target date for entering into a partnership is October 2025. The partnership will be subject to further approval by both boards and the relevant shareholders.

Paradigm Housing Group is a housing association with almost 17,000 homes in Buckinghamshire, Bedfordshire and Hertfordshire. Settle Group is a housing association with over 10,000 homes across Hertfordshire, Bedfordshire, Buckinghamshire and South Cambridgeshire.

The proposed merger accompanies the announcement today that Paradigm Homes Charitable Housing Association intends to acquire nearly 3,500 homes from L&Q.  Paradigm and Settle are proposing to join together to form a new organisation which would own and manage 30,000 homes, mainly in Buckinghamshire, Hertfordshire and Bedfordshire.

The partnership will be subject to registration by the Financial Conduct Authority.

Paradigm is registered as a charitable community benefit society under the Co-operative and Community Benefit Society Act 2014 with registered number 28844R and as a registered provider with the Social Housing Regulator with registered number L4215.

The Issuer is registered as a charitable community benefit society under the Co-operative and Community Benefit Society Act 2014 with registered number 27789R and as a registered provider with the Social Housing Regulator with registered number LH4138.

Settle is registered as a charitable community benefit society under the Co-operative and Community Benefit Society Act 2014 with registered number 30003R and as a registered provider with the Social Housing Regulator with registered number L4370.

For further information, please contact:

Nicola Ewen
